title: Phenomenon 366
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 366: "Crimson Trauma"}} {{Content Warning|bad_content=Mental trauma, depictions of extreme and surreal gore|page= {{Quote|The entire place looked organic, almost as if it was all one living thing.|Unknown wanderer|color=#121212}}{{Quote|The entire place looked organic, almost as if it was all one living thing.|Unknown wanderer|color=#e6e6e6}} {{Blurimage|file=CrimsonNightmare2.jpg|caption=An artwork depicting one of a wanderer's alleged visions caused by the phenomenon.|hovertext=Crimson tower}} ==Description== '''Phenomenon 366''', popularly known as 'Crimson Trauma', is a rare yet dangerous cognitive phenomenon that afflicts certain wanderers who are suffering from severe trauma. The phenomenon starts at random times for these wanderers regardless of how long they have had their trauma for. Nonetheless, the affected wanderers will begin seeing a "nightmarish" location in their dreams and, in some cases, in brief visions of it when awake. Over a time of roughly 24-96 hours, the dreams and visions will worsen, with the latter becoming guaranteed to occur and lasting longer. At the end of this period, the victim will inexplicably enter a permanent coma where all of their conscious brain activity ceases, essentially leaving the body in an unconscious vegetative state. It is commonly assumed that, upon entering this coma, the victim's consciousness is transferred to the place in their visions. Those that believe in this place's existence generally accept that it is a metaphysical level, referring to it as the phenomenon's nickname, 'Crimson Trauma'. The only known way of escaping the phenomenon is the healing of one's trauma; even partially accomplishing this is often enough to save one from its effects. However, due to the nature of trauma, this is often impossible. According to each report from wanderers under the effects of '''Phenomenon 366''', the "level" is made out of a red substance with an appearance and texture ranging from smooth to fleshy and organic. Giant blocky structures that typically appear to be "growing out of the fleshy ground" of the level are prevalent and often apparently boundless, with some witnesses mentioning being inside these structures with no discernible way out. The fleshy texture is more common on the ground but can be found elsewhere, and vice-versa with the smooth texture frequently observed in the structures. The consistency of space between the inside and outside of structures varies and there is no definite 'outside' in the level. {{Blurimage|file=EscherianStairsButItsNightmareFuel.jpg|caption=Another artwork displaying one of the same affected wanderer's alleged visions.|hovertext=Escherian nightmare}} The overall architecture of the level's structures is generally chaotic, though usually traversable. One of the predominant features of the structures are archways of varying sizes, varying from human-sized to unnaturally large and typically being chaotically-positioned throughout the structures. These archways often do not serve their structural purpose and sometimes seemingly even act as 'windows'. Another notable aspect of the unconfirmed level is its infrequent light sources. While light is normally mysteriously available regardless of whether any discernible light sources are nearby, sometimes illuminating their surroundings are softly-glowing yellow lights. These lights can appear as surreal lamps and lamp posts and can even emit from the sky in some visions. Crimson beings have occassionally been reported to appear in visions. While there is a stark lack of information on them, they have been described to look "frightening" and "surreal" by witnesses. They typically do not interact with or even acknowledge the existence of any wanderers encountering them in visions, though they have in some cases allegedly noticed their observers and reacted mostly with curiosity and even more rarely with unprovoked hostility. Fortunately, these entities are unable to physically interact with wanderers experiencing the visions, possibly explaining why most choose not to attack.
